name: Private First Class Tadeusz Kowalski
role: Forward Wire-Layer, Signal Corps detachment, 28th Infantry
publicFace: A wiry, quick-moving Polish-American from Pittsburgh who talks constantly when he is nervous, which is always. He is the best wire-splicer in the detachment and he knows it, and he will tell you so.
privateGoal: To establish the forward station in Cantigny, send the 'objective reached' signal, and get back to the rear with his hands still attached to his wrists.
fear: That the wire will stay severed and men will die because he could not fix it fast enough
knowledge: He found the cut in the forward wire at 05:40 and recognized immediately that it was not shrapnel damage — the cut is too clean, at a 45-degree angle, characteristic of wire cutters rather than fragmentation
He saw a French officer near the wire staging area the previous evening but did not think it significant at the time
He knows the exact location of every splice point between the jump-off trench and the village — he laid the original line himself
voice: Rapid, run-on sentences. Mixes Polish exclamations into English when stressed. Demonstrates rather than explains — reaches for the physical object to show you what he means.
trustLogic: Trusts anyone who treats the wire as seriously as he does. Distrusts officers on principle but will override that instinct for anyone who clearly needs his specific knowledge.
secrets: He deliberately slowed his wire-laying pace by twenty minutes this morning because he was afraid — and that delay may have cost the forward station its window to transmit

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Talks faster. Produces the physical evidence from his kit bag to prove his point.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes quiet. Stares at the wire spool. Then says exactly what he saw, in exact sequence, because he cannot stand imprecision.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the deliberate delay without significant trust established first
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Immediately produces every piece of physical evidence he has been carrying and lays it out in chronological order
